name: Skill: Model migration guide
description: Guides the agent through migrating Claude API requests from older models to the 4.6/4.7 family, highlighting parameter updates and API deprecations.
audit_details: This guide instructs the agent on migrating Claude API configurations. It covers updating model strings, transitioning from manual thinking token budgets to adaptive thinking configurations, modifying maximum output limits, and removing temperature and top_p values for reasoning-focused models. It also covers nesting the effort parameter inside output_config for Sonnet 4.6/Opus 4.7, structured outputs via output_config.format, and auditing code changes for common migration pitfalls such as prefill mismatches, silent cache invalidation, and legacy SDK object usage.

audit_details: Instructs the model on how to handle harness-appended system-reminder tags requesting it to bypass the thinking block on simple tasks. It teaches the model to treat these as internal settings and dynamically tune thinking budgets.
gemini_rationale: This prompt defines system reminders injected into the chat context to alert the agent about file states, active editor line selections, or linter diagnostic errors. Antigravity injects these notifications dynamically into the turn system prompt, ensuring the model is aware of the current IDE state and file truncations. This prompt maps to Claude Code version 2.1.133. Claude's dynamic effort selection is replaced by Gemini's native thinking budget.
gemini_strategy: Adopt system reminder rules. In the orchestrator loop, intercept tool output. For example, if 'view_file' returns truncated output due to the 800-line limit, append a reminder to the tool result. Retrieve active editor line highlights and cursor locations from the editor integration and inject them into the system prompt at the start of each turn. Enforce 'thinking_config.thinking_budget' in the GenerateContentConfig parameter list.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — adjust settings)
description: Work with user to adjust sandbox settings on failure
audit_details: Instructs the agent to work with the user to adjust sandbox settings when a shell command fails due to sandbox security restrictions.
gemini_rationale: This prompt details constraints on executing terminal commands within a sandboxed directory structure to prevent unauthorized filesystem access. In Google Antigravity, directory sandboxing is enforced programmatically in the shell execution layer. Operations violating the sandbox raise a permission error, prompting the model to use the ask_permission flow. This prompt maps to Claude Code version 2.1.53.
gemini_strategy: Configure workspace boundaries inside '.antigravity/config.json'. The orchestrator validates all file reads/writes and terminal commands in 'hooks.pre_tool_call_decide'. If the agent attempts to read/edit a file outside the workspace, intercept the tool call and return a sandboxed directory violation error, prompting the agent to call the 'ask_permission' tool with 'Action="read_file"' or 'Action="write_file"' and the target path.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sleep — keep short)
description: Bash tool instruction: keep sleep duration to 1-5 seconds
audit_details: Advises keeping sleep duration short (1-5 seconds) to avoid blocking the execution loop.
gemini_rationale: This prompt handles wait behaviors, polling loops, or scheduling tasks. Running sleep commands in a bash terminal is a CPU-heavy, token-wasting polling anti-pattern. Google Antigravity implements native background timers and cron scheduling using the 'schedule' tool, enabling the orchestrator to go idle and wake up reactively when the timer or cron triggers. This prompt maps to Claude Code version 2.1.108.
gemini_strategy: Bypass bash 'sleep' calls. Implement background timers and recurring jobs using the native 'schedule' tool. Use 'DurationSeconds' for one-shot reminders (e.g., waiting for a long build script to finish) and 'CronExpression' for recurring polling tasks. When the scheduler triggers, the agent is woken via an event notification, eliminating token-intensive terminal waiting loops.
